---
title: "Week 9 — Probability as risk and diagnosis"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

How do chance, risk, and testing interact?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Probability as risk; conditional probability through diagnostic
tables; sensitivity, specificity, false positives, and base rates.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Read a diagnostic two-by-two table and compute sensitivity,
specificity, and predictive values.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab; screening-test case study.

## Primary source basis

No direct IMS chapter. Build from ISLBS Ch 2–3 once mirrored, plus
custom diagnostic-table vignettes.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

This week is **custom-built** in v1. ISLBS supplies most of the raw
material; custom screening-test cases anchor the practical framing.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Interpret probability as risk and uncertainty.
- [ ] Use two-way tables for diagnostic testing.
- [ ] Explain false positives and false negatives.
- [ ] Describe why rare events make positive tests harder to
      interpret.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
